vocabulary.name: Google Cloud Healthcare API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Key terms and concepts used in the Google Cloud Healthcare API

terms.definition: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources - a standard for exchanging healthcare information electronically, defined by HL7.
A managed FHIR data repository within a Cloud Healthcare dataset that stores and indexes FHIR resources.
Health Level 7 version 2 - the most widely deployed healthcare messaging standard for exchanging clinical data between hospital systems.
Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine - a standard for medical imaging data storage and transmission.
A set of RESTful services for medical imaging: WADO-RS (retrieve), STOW-RS (store), QIDO-RS (query).
Protected Health Information - individually identifiable health information protected under HIPAA.
The process of removing or transforming PHI and PII from healthcare data to protect patient privacy.
Patient authorization for the use and disclosure of their health information, managed via the Consent Management API.
A top-level container in Cloud Healthcare API that holds FHIR stores, HL7v2 stores, DICOM stores, and consent stores.
A set of open specifications to integrate apps with Electronic Health Records using FHIR and OAuth2.
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act - US law establishing national standards for protecting patient health information.
Google Cloud Pub/Sub - used for event-driven notifications when FHIR resources are created, updated, or deleted.
The ability to export FHIR resources or HL7v2 messages to Google BigQuery for analytics.
Cloud Data Loss Prevention - Google Cloud service integrated with the de-identification API for advanced PHI inspection.
A consent enforcement model where access to data is governed by attributes defined in consent policies.
A FHIR batch or transaction containing multiple FHIR operations executed atomically, limited to 4,500 entries.
